provenance.p: Bequeathed by Edward Harley to his widow and their daughter; they sold them to the nation under the Act of Parliament which established the British Museum.

revisionDesc.change: Corrected line numbering and small error on line 779.
Moved notes nested in lg/ab elements out of these to stand alone.

Corrected "AND:baail" to "AND:baailer" on line 236. Change 1.02 reversed on basis that 'ayn' form dominates in this MS (e.g. 'mayn', 'mayntenant', 'payn', 'vilayn', etc), therefore supporting 'plantayn' over 'plantain'.
Corrected supplied text 'plantain' (line 569) in accordance with the AND entry, since 'plantayn' (the old entry) is not attested elsewhere in the MS.
Amended format of pbs and added AND labels to key attributes where missing.
Numerous corrections and resolutions of doubts; ready for committal.
Updated and resolved a number of readings during BL visit.
Edited 'collation' to incorporate findings from in-person consultation of the MS
Incorporated back into 'texts' folder; fixed two extra spaces " > on ll. 477, 987 (in XML).
First pass of encoding complete. Handed over to Aida.

body.note: Although appearing as fol. 15r in the current binding, this badly faded and torn folio marks the start of the text. A few identifiable words, transcribed here, confirm this.
body.p:
est le tretiz qe m fist a ma dame
enphauntz fraunceis
body.note: Only the second half of lines from the top half of folio 15v are visible due to damage. The text proper evidently starts ten lines down the page, but only the odd word is legible.
body.p:
de bricer & bracer
ruer & de charet de
Ffaucher, carier, muer
edefier, Vviaunde atirer
est de chescun apris
lieux deuetzdevetz parler
enphauntz puissent sauoirsavoir
nt dire moun & ma, toun
le & la.

lg.l: tempsThe few surviving words on this page enable the reader to identify the first four lines of the verse text, starting with Femme ke aproche soun tens (quoted from MS C). In this instance, we have used a different numbering system based on letters, to avoid arbitrary counting of such fragmentary and isolated single-word scraps within the surviving substantive body of the text.
un ventrer mydwyff

body.note: Folio 1r is almost entirely missing, save for a marginal stub. Four lines around halfway down begin Pur l'o[...], Ton [...], Et q[...] and Et, suggesting a close correspondence with lines 25-28 in MS C. There appear to be a number of blank lines between the final line of Infancy and Early Childhood and the start of the Parts of the Body section below it (visible is Faites, the opening word of line 31 in MS C.
Folio 1v is almost entirely missing, save for a marginal stub.
The 'Parts of the Body' section is acephalous.

lg.l: Del oil eghe est tout saufe le purnele appel of þe egh
Si le paupere eghlydde soit bone &et beale fair.
En les paupers eghlyddes sount les ciees,
Amont abown les oils sont les surciees·
Le nees doit auoiravoir par reson
Deux naires thyrls &et vnun tendron brygge,
Vous auetzavetz le leuerelevere hare, le leurelevre lyppe,
La lyuerlyver pund auxi &et le lyuerlyver buke.
Le lyuerlyver lippe· est qen q'enclost closis les dentz tethe,
La leuerlever heir soi teint en boys wodd dedeinz witin.
La lyuerlyver punde sert seruysservys a marchandisez,
Le lyurelyvre buk nous apprent lerys clargiesclargiés/.
En bouche mowth amont abown par palet place of þe mowthe
Tasterez taste voz choses thynges orde fule ou nett klene.
Et les dames sont ententifs tentit
Pur bien lauerlaver was lours gyngyuesgyngyves gomes.
LenchesonL'encheson est de certein:
QiilsQi ils le font do pur bone aleyn aynde.
Dont le fraunceis est comon.
Desoutz undyr la langue tonge est la fourchelle chauilbonechavilbone/,

VnUn furchefurché hokbaneUnusually, this MS has un furché rather than os furché, with the second word only being the subject of glossing. It is therefore unsatisfactory to use AND os_1 as a headword, with the compound os furché. The MED has hokbone listed under the headword hok. en fraunceis leml'em appelle/,
De / deintzDedeintz la gorge throte est la gargait throtebolle/,
Et pluis par foundparfound beneth/ si gist lyes la rate mydrede/.
Si ad derer la wene fex· faxfexfax en cole neke//
Et du sage &et du foil.This line has been inserted in the margin with a cross.
En cheif auauntavaunt enest la frountayn,
La souerayn leuresoverayn levre ouerover lippe &et la suzaynneþer lippe.The two glosses on line 28 have been inverted, but are labelled 'a' and 'b' by the scribe to indicate the correct order.
En bouche mouthe sount/ les messelers axilteght
Et dens foreins forteghtThe gloss is executed over foreins./, si tu les queres.
Au cole auetzavetz vnun fosselet pytt in þe neke.
Desoutz la lange est la filet skale.
Et pur estre parfit en parole/
Chescun orail þe ere se ad molet dewelappe//.
Et par cacanole rym est ceruelcervel harnes nett clene/.
Desoutz orail ere auetzavetz gernon þe hok of þe ere.
As espaules shuldyrs auetzavetz blason shuldirban.
Desoutz les braces auetzavetz huesselle armhole/.
Parmy la chare gist l'esselle axiltre/.
La chare flehs chiuachechivache rydys en la chare carte
Dount les vnsuns sum fount lour eschare scorn.

body.note: Only the first letter of certain lines is visible, the rest of folio 3r having been cut or torn out.
Only the final letters of an addition or gloss are visible, the rest of folio 3v having been cut or torn out.
Folio 4r has been cut or torn out.
Folio 4v has been cut or torn out.

lg.l: Pent hengeth la lanier lanʒer qe vous attache fast
Les deux chaucez hosys sauns frouncier wrythyng.
Et pur naturelment parler to speke,
Il ad lanier lanʒer &et laner
Dount wharof ieojeo vous dirra la manere.
Homme proprement dit laner,
Et faucon est dit l'espervier sparehawke
Qe daungerous est a nurir
Et qe a clayms ne voet venir.
Et tout auxi dit homme laner harlot
VnUn mal ribaude pautoner
Qi se retrait witdrawys par coardie
A fet fra dede ou il ne doit my noght.
Ceo est la propre volaunce
Par dreit autoriteautorité de franceFraunce.
VncorUncor il ad de les aultres draps
Ore eincz vous nomay pas/.
Il ad ma chemys sarke &et ma cote,
Mon surcot auxi &et ma cloke,
Mon tabert/ &et mon chapelet,
Mon corn horn &et mon cornett whystyll/.
Mettez du on le chaperon hude, couerezcoverez le chief hede/,
Tachez feste voz botons derechief. agaynwarde/
DunD'un corei thwang vous ceyntez gyrde.
Ne die ieojeo pas enceyntez wyt/ chylde,
Car femme est par homme enceynte wit shilde
Et dund'un corei thwange est eil ceynte gyrdyd.
De la Ceinturecenture gyrdyl le pendant pendaunt/
Passe parmy thurgh le mordant bokyll.
Et ensi doit le hardilion þe tunge
Passer par treu hole de subilion elsyn.
VncorUncor affert it fals de deuerdever
Ma burse &et mon aguler nedylhowe,

Lines 76a and 76b are inserted in the margin, and indicated in the text with a nota. They record an alternative discussion of the word cotel, as found in MSS BO, and we have therefore numbered them separately from the rest of the text.LaLe cotel oueové le gaynThe gloss thegh on line 76a appears to offer sheath as an additional sense to those recorded in MED, though related in sense – all concern different kinds of covering or enclosure (e.g. the membrane covering the brain; a box, chest; a cover for a chest; an enclosed piece of land). thegh bele,
Et bien trenchaunt la lemele blade.
Le cotel knyf auxi &et mon espeespé swerde
Dont l'enfez childe est affaiteaffaité lerynde.
De le cotel fait assuer towypto wyp
Et lespel'espé fait a terder to wype.
Et pees &et mayns auxi suez clene,
Mais le nees enmuchez clene.
Et si meistre mystir y yar aueigneaveigne befaul·,
CoueintCoveint le coof cap &et le peigne cambe.
Mais si vous centez l'espeespé swerde
Qe soit bien acerreacerré stelyd,
Aincz est trenchant darme et dure dull vel harde.
Ne fait a saker draw owt de la forour scauberde.
Forour scauberde dit homme del espeespé swerde,
Mais de paynSee AND pane: fur, skin used for lining (a hood or cloak etc.).serra furefuré furryd
Mantel/, cape/, ou garnement/;
Qi altre vous dit mesprent mystakys.
Et ceo ne fait my a vblierublier
Pur voz enphauntz affaiter areche.
Ore nowe pur nourrer vos enphauntz childir
Qe vous facez come sachant wyse/
Al diner &et al sopier
Pur bien les gouernergoverner gouernegoverne
Joefne enphaunt ȝong childe vous teint haldys la mayne hande
Au maytn enuersenvers le payne brede,
VnUn bribe lump donques lui donez,
Ou vnun leche shyef si pluis nauetzn'avetz.
Mes bot si vous lui donetz des oefs heggys,
Issint so atirez arayeȝ a son oeps huse.
Oustez putowtput owt l'escale shell eincz qilq'il hume sup,

LL'estreinThe French lemma estrein, otherwise unattested, appears to be a calque on Middle English stren, defined by the MED (sense 2b) as the germinal vesicle in the yolk of an egg. strene auxi &et l'aubume whyte.
Si lui donetz le moel ȝolke
Qe a seyn hale home est bone morsel.
Mes le gerynThe word geryn is unattested elsewhere. We have treated it as a variant of germin, though the gloss would then be inaccurate. rym vous en oustez,
Qe a nul est prue profet, ceo eut sachez wyt.
Des poumes apils vous di ensement also/
La maner &et la feitement/.
Poumes maungent pur ceo qilsq'ils ayment luf/
Et de droit enphauntz les clament cryis/.
Oustez l'estik stalke &et la parure parynge,
Si lui donetz le morsure.
La pepynger kyrnel engettez castoutecast oute.
Si les pepyngez plauntez sette.
Bele fair douce swet enphaunt, pur apprendre
En fraunceis deuetzdevetz awt entendre
De chescun manere assembleassemblé togydyr
De bestez qe dieuDieu ad fourmefourmé
Et des oyseaux ensement also/
Parler speke deit awt homme proprement.
Primes ou cerfs hertes sont assemblez
VnUn herde est appelez,
Des grues cranes auxi vnun herde,
Et de gryuesgryves feldefars saunz ·h·.h. erde;
NieNié des fesantz, couecové des perdrez partryges,
Dame des alowes larkys/, trip des berbesberbés shyp,
BeueieBeveie des herons/, pipepipé des oyseaux byrdys,
Soundre de porkes &et de Stourneauxstourneaux,Line 132 has been added in the margin, with its position relative to the main text indicated through a marginal 'nota'.
Grelie des gelyns hens, tourbe des cercilz telys,
Lure des faucons &et de pucels damesels.
Et pur lurer, lui fauconer
Pur reclamer au poyn noef son meistre,
Soit a destre ryghthanderyght hande ou senestre lefte.
Car pusselle maydyn sciet saunz iuperjuper crying
Les gentils faucons a lureralurer.
Eschiel sheltron dit homme de batale.
Fuson dit homme de vif aumaile quyke best·.
Harriz dit homme des poleyns coltys,
Folie dit homme de vilayns carlys.
Somme des bleez, some des beinz,
Counceil dapostelled'apostelle of þe pape/, parlement du Roy ;
Jeo loo rede qe vous apprendrez en bone foy.
Bruy des barons doit homme nomer name,
Frape des clerkes de droit deuerdever.
Arraie dit homme des cheuelerschevelers knyghtys,
Route dit homme des esquiers squiers.
Et des ribaudes harlots ensement route,
Et des boefs/ oxin &et burgois burges auxi, saunz doute.
Compaigny cumpani des dames ladys dirrez,
Et des owes geys ensi freez sal//dosal do.
Mote des cheins hundys en venerie huntynge,
Et de cornir blawe, qi qe l'em man die.
Car tiel fraunceis il teint de droit/.
Le meistre baudement le troit drawys/.
Mes parpur plusparfitementplus parfitement
Parler deuauntdevaunt bone gent/,
Il· y a· ventre blawe/, sufflere blawe &et corner blawe
Come la reson fait assauoirassavoir towyteto wyte.
Le feu fyer sufflie blawys le quystron.
Le vent wynde vente blawys parmy le busshom buske
Mais venour hunter proprement corneie blawys
Chace hunt quant prent de pure clene preie.
Le solail sun lust shynys en temps de yueryver wynter esteesté somerThe scribe has added the marginal note: esté : somer, presumably as a counterpart to the mention of winter in the text.
Et vent fait frillomsSubstantive frillom is not currently attested in AND, but is presumably cognate with the adjective frillus (cold, chilly). charply endurer.
Des bestes le diuersementdiversement.
LenchesonL'encheson dund'un &et de chescun ilkanilk an,
Solonc after ceo qe lour nature done gyfis.
Homme parle spekys, vrse·urse braie bere brays,
Ore a mesour vous dirra.
Lion rumyst rumys/ &et coudre/croustcoudre croust/. hasyl waggys
Putoys fulmarde le agnel lame affraie affrays.
Quant le venour hunter quert pray.
Et ane dun en marriz jaroil kekys,
Il· y· a iaroiljaroil &et garel·.
La difference dire vous voil.
Lui ane iaroiljaroil cryes en riuerriver
Si homme de faucon lui quer
Mais deuauntdevaunt ville en guerre
Affiche homme garoil trap en terre
Pur la barbicane kyrnel· defendre.
Al assaut qe home voet rendre.
Si qe la porte ne perderienperde rien noght lese thynge
Si lelé guerrours teignent bien halde wele.
Columbe gerist dufe curlys &et cok chaunt crawys,
ChiuaulxChivaulx hynnyst &et alowe chauntLine 197 has been executed in the margin, and incorporated into the text through a nota.
Coleour snake proprement regail hessys·,
Troi grundelle sow grunteth quaunt drage draf quert sekys/.
Faucon tercel en plumison danid feirt strikys/.
Gelyn hen proprement patille clokys
Quant il poyne warpys en garden ou en ville,Lines 206-208 have been executed in the margin, and incorporated into the text through a nota.
Car en frauncesfrauncés ad tiel estiel
Qe Gelyngelyn vppeuppé coppid pown et patylle.
Mes l'estrynal pipe &et la nascye rowp
Desturbent q'ils ne poient mye
Et le desplumer multyngeThis predates existing MED mouting by a century. ensement/
Les affeblest trop ower souentsovent/.
Et quaunt gelyn hen grael greselde choleus clokkys
Et de trepeller hetcheyn fait la noyse noyse,
Des oefs eggys dorrez salgyfsal gyf germoez rymmyde,
Nompas qe sount estergulez rotyn.
Ja soient sez oefs/ taunt couuzcovuz:
Poucyn chekyn naueran'avera, silss'ils soient caduz falle.
Et qi trope se auauntavaunt saunz reson
A la gelyn est compaignon.
Qe pluis se auauntavaunt pur vnun oefe
Qe pur le arrour tyllynge fait dus le boefe ox·/.
Mais pur rehercer ma matier
La diuersitediversité fait a dire
Qe il ad gelyn graelegraelé spekkyd,
Vache cow ou veel calf lorellelorellé garde,
ChiualChival veiron varund al front paillepaillé sternyd,
Tup &et cheuereuxchevereux gaytys gaillegaillé brukyd.
VncorUncor pur le bestiar
Assetz ennohe pluis y ad affair: to make The gloss appears to misinterpret affair, topic, as a faire, to do.
Berbitz balei bletes/, dame baleThe gloss appears to have mistaken dame (lady) for dame (doe). doo bleteth·,
Espicer prent sez mercez de bale·,
Et de balys besum homme baylly swepys.
Mundez la sale, eincz qe la ionchiejonchie strewitstrew it·.
Par trop veiler wakynge homme baale ganys·.
A son seruantservant sa chose baille betakys.
Vous alez de souche hassok a souche/
Come· baaler ganynge va· de bouche mowth a bouche.
ApresAprés dormer homme ses preches'espreche rax.
Le peschour fysher· en viver pesche fyshys
Ou de sa raiez net, ou de sa hesche huke·.

l.note: The bottom section of folio 9r is missing, likely containing lines equivalent to C 341-42.

lg.l: Quaunt pouerepovere femme mesne lede la tresche dawns,
Pluis vaudroit en mayn la besche spade,
Car il nadn'ad ou se abesche bowe
Ne de payn, bribe lump, ne lesche shyueshyve.
Son chaol whelp le pael pan leche lykkys.
Ne prent garde de sale ne fresche.
Ore donetz le chaol whelp a flater lape,
Qe lesche lykkys la ruserusé dew del erber gyrs,
Et fuez fleȝe flatour qi sciet flater
Et des gentz l'espeluperThe use of espeluper as a substantive is not recorded in AND. bakbytynge;
Qe en ton chaperon ne voet lesser leueleve
VnUn poton mote, tauunt ad te/te scher dere
Numpas toy, mes ton aueraver
QilQ'il desyre de toy aueraver.
Veiez, cy here vient deuauntdevaunt vous
VnUn tresbeal chiualerchivaler rous rede.
Sur vnun chiualchival soer est iaja mountemounté.
Eschu shelde de goules ad pourtepourté.Line 261 been executed in the margin, and incorporated into the text through a nota.
VnUn launce spere rouge rede en lunel'une mayn,
Et de vyn vermail rede lautrel'autre pleyn ful,
Qe ne maunge point noght de pesson fessh
SilS'il del haranc heryng sore noun bot.The bottom of folio 9v is torn, making it impossible to tell whether any lines have been lost.
iJeo veie vener vnun roygne qwene saunz vnun Royroy

Pur vneune rayn frosk fair derayThe gloss aray may be admissible with a generic or unspecific sense of conduct, behavior, though its antonym derai (disorder, disturbance; noise, outcry) would better fit the specific sense of the French term. aray
Qi enmy in þe middis le roialme reme le Royroy
En vnun reume fure soet en vnun rey net.
VnUn vilayn veint en ma forer hedland;
Jeo crie qilq'il fuist forener foreyn.
Il dist q'ilq'il voet forier forrey
Le leuerelevere qilq'il vist forueirforveir farrey.
Le bedale mon seignur le count heryl
Au Seniscallseniscall ma dame counte telde
Qe venir vous facez le viscont sherafe
Et lui rende loial· accompt acownt.
Le cotelle est darmredarmré dulle au poynt
Qe ne perce ne ne poynt,
Aincz bote faute toutditz a chescun poynt
Ne ne face vnqoreunqore poynt.
oOre aloms as prees medys &et as chaumps feldys/
Pur enfourmer noz enphauntz.
De fauche lee/ fauchez vnun aunndyen swaþe de pree mede.
Du faucil sekyl·· ciez vnun iaueljavel handful de bleblé corn.
Voz iauelsjavels handfuls en garbes shawys liez byndys·,
Et en trosseaux stukys voz garbez shawys mettez.
VnUn varroit reed de poys peys suisrachezsuis rachez
Et les feaves benys du varoit liez.
Mais nestn'est mestier qe vous dirrons
De tout manere de bleblé noums,
De segle ry/, d'orge barle/, ou de fourment wheet/,
Qe comons sont as toutz gentz.
Mais dautred'autre semail sed trop y crest growys
Qe as cotescote is not attested with this sense, nor as a variant of cultiver, in AND or DEAF. tylmene assetz displest/.
Le yuerayyveray drawk crest &et le betel darnel,
Le hazon stare auxi &et le neel popil,
Le blauerolblaverol blawede &et le maluemalve auxi,
Caroil carlow &et autres qe ieojeo vous die,
Car moult y aueroitaveroit dont affair
Qi toutz semails sedys vous deuoitdevoit retraier.
Mais quaunt temps est decarierde carier,
Vos charetz facez charger.
Sez chiualschivals doit le charetter
De son riort whyp demesner lede.
Le charetter ad son riort,
Mais le charuer plowman l'agalon gad port/.
En les graungez· voz bleez mowez,
De horsDehors la graunge vos bleez tassez stakys,
Car vnun mowe est dit en graunge/
Et vnun tasse mowe de horsdehors la graunge/.
Molion stake of hay dit est ceo questqu'est de feyn,
Et tasse est ceo qestq'est de greyne.
En graunge vous gardez de arestez awn,
En chaumpez· vos· bleez de sauuagesauvage bestes.
En sale chauntez vous les gestez
Pur vblierublier griefs ou molestez.
Mais si vous priez gentz a vos festez,
Priez ceaux qi sont de enquestez
Pur estre appris par yceauxy cestezycestez
DeschuerD'eschuer les malesices qe sount si prestez.
Quant vostre bleblé corn est bien batu beryd,
Puis venteventé wyndow &et puis molu grun,
Qe par la moudre grondynge deueintdeveint faryne mele
Ceo qenq'en greyn fuist huy matyn·,
Et de greyn iaja deueintdeveint floure
Par le bolenge bultyngclathe &et le pestour.
Par la bolenge bultyngclath est seuereseveré/Lines 328 and 329 are written out here as a single line, with a slash added to separate them.
laLa floure &et furfre bran iaja demuredemuré dwels.
A voz chiualschivals la furfur donez.
Eaw water teuueteuve warm oueové floure medlez.
Vostre past ensi pestrez bakȝebak ȝe,
Et dund'un rastour ryb vostre auge trogh mondez.
Mais il y ad rastel &et rasture
Qe seruentservent de diuersediverse meistre·.
Tauunt come feyns sount en preez
Est le rastel rake maynez.
Le pastour ad en mayn rastelle rak/,
Mais rastur ryb fait laugel'auge þe trogh beal.
Quant la paste doughe al auge troghe ahert cleuysclevys,
La rastur ryb· de ceo lour sert ceruyscervys/.
Mais ceo est restuelAND records restuel, here 'rest (for a lance'), under the same lemma as rastel, seen above as 'rake'. reste de launce/,
Come pert en guerre/ ou en chaunceSee AND chance, sense 3: danger, hazard.
Encountre son aduerseradverser
Qe relement latly lui voet bien fair.
Mais pur pour drede de son enmy
Ne voet/ tarier eincz· qilq'il soit muscy hyde,

body.note: Approximately half of folio 12r has been torn, obscuring the latter part of most lines. Where possible, the end of the word 'in-progress' has been provided in the interpretive edition, but no attempt has been made to reconstruct the remainder of the missing lines.

lg.l: Car nestn'est si long par vnun teise cubet
Qe lui fait/ clocher halt saunz ease·
Taunt vist swyftely lui fert en la g
QilQ'il est fait sicome vnun p
Ore au pester bakynge redderons
Ou plus apprendre y purrons.
Eschaufez le fourne de
Pur defaute de liter stree.
Liter &et liter sont diuersediverses,
Descordauntz dient les
Le fauchour liter fauche.
Pur ease en liter horsbere homme
Mais litere proprement
En pure fraunceFraunce dit
Pail &et pale sont
Quant de greyn
Et si de pail
Pernez donques
Et quant le forn
De pele soit

body.note: Approximately half of folio 12v has been torn, obscuring the former part of most lines. Where possible, the start of the word 'in-progress' has been provided in the interpretive edition, but no attempt has been made to reconstruct the remainder of the missing lines.

lg.l: payn brede est enfourneen fourne

l.note: The French word to which this English gloss corresponds has been lost to damage. Assuming that the line here follows the readings of other manuscripts (from MS C, line 459: Ne serra pas trop nounchaliere), it is plausible that the full English word is taryenere, a nominalisation of the verb tarien: to delay in acting, tarry, procrastinate. The noun tarienere, with the sense of one who tarries, would be a new attestation for the MED.

lg.l: vostre linois lynsede,
vostre kanois hempsed,
molt beal
er
Et puis apresaprés le cerencez hekyl,
Et dounques al canoil roke vus purueiezpurveiez.
Et si ne faut mais fusille spyndille noun
Qe a mesure deuiserondeviseron.
Saunz le veroil whorl qe a ceo suffist/
Ne scaysçay si vous sauera dist/.
Mais il ad vncqoreuncqore diuersitediversité
Dount nestn'est chescun bien auiseavisé.
De trois choses siert le fusille spyndil·/.
Le file thred est filiefilié spun du fusille/,
Et fu fier de caillon flynstan fiert fusille fyryryn/,
Et blee est molu par fusil.Line 400 has been executed in the margin, and incorporated into the text through a nota.
Quaunt vostre file est iaja filefilé spun
Et vosttre fusille/ est empleemplé fulfild/,
As voiders garnwyndils/ ore alez·
Et vostre file la voidez·.
Qe fait/ ore/ dame hydeHyde
VnUn lusselle clew/ du voidre garnwyndil· voide/.
A· trahille reel· coueintcoveint donques aler
Vostre filefilé a traheler reel.
Et puis coueintcoveint a vaunder wynde,
Come de filefilé est la maner
Pur waunder apresaprés la trahille rele/.
Et puis apresaprés/ testier wef le voil.
la·LaThe first letter of this line has been erased. tistresse webstar quant perula l'a warpys/
Tauntost les tremes spulys waundra salwynde·sal wynde.
Ore vous ay dit/ quantque· appent/
Forsque· de la lame slay seulement/.The third minim on the 'm' has been added above.
PPuis qe de ceo suffist/
leLe fraunceis qe vous ay dist/,
Ore serroit bone assauoirassavoir
Coment homme doit brazer to brew &et brecer·
A· la maner qe homme fait seruoiseservoise
Pur fair nous &et noz gestezIs this French geste, with the sense of race, family, lineage, or could it be a code-switched gest, an invited guest? a ease.
Alumez, a celleauncelle, le frinol,Lines 423-424 has been executed in the margin.
Et puis maungesmaungés de bracol.
VneUne cowe fatt large primes pernez
Et vostre orge barly enfoundrez stepe.
Et quaunt il est bien enfoundreenfoundré stepyd,
Et la eawe soit descoueledescovelé runowtrun owt,
Mountez donques a vnun soler,
Si le facez· bien balier swepyd,
Et illeoques coucherez le blees corn
Tantque soit bien germinez cumin,
Et de celle hure þat tyme appellerez
Breis malt ce qe ainz bleblé nomas.
Le Breezbreez donques des mains mouetz
En monceaus hepys ou en rengeez rawys,
Et puis/ le portez· en vnun corbail lepe·
Pur enseccher dry au torail kyln/,
Car corbail lepe ou corbaillon skepe
SeruirServir vous doit a fuison.
Et puis sera les bres molu grun
Et de eaw chaude warm bien en buenbu sokyd·.
Si le lerrez decurer lat it ryn atauunt
Hors del couerelcoverel fatte mayntenant anon,
Desques en vnun coauge troghe bealLine 445 has been added in the margin, and inserted using a nota.
ApresAprés le soioursojour en couerelcoverel fatte,

Tauntque qeThe second (abbreviated) half of 'Tauntque' appears to have been overwritten over the top of a separate (and subsequently redundant) 'que' abbreviation. This is one of a number of revisions made to the original text on folio 14r. vntounto la braceresse brewster entent
QilQ'il ad bercy growta talent.
Et puis le berzise prendra,
De fourment ou dorged'orge qilq'il a·.
Et par le berm e le bersize
Dont homme se sutille ad mys,
Par droit deuerdever de bracerye brewyng
Dount tut deuiserdeviser ne puisse mye
De buler wel ne le purgeler to scum/.
Mais pur diuersementdiversement parler,
Le serf seruant·servant embule shamys le seignur
Quaunt il retrahit son honour,

Mais tout de cy dartd'art en artLines 458a and 458b have been added in the margin, and inserted using a nota. As at 76a and 76b above, they appear to offer an alternative version of the text, and specifically of the mention of brewing as an art; therefore they have not been included in the main line count. The placement, however, is confusing: in the version of this passage found in MSS CGOT, they come before the lines discussing drunkenness found below here, at 464 onward. This section in MS B is closely similar (it is the only other extant MS to include the wordplay on buler), but includes neither these two interpolated lines nor the passage on drunkenness.
Atirer deuonsdevons de chechun part.
Par la ou neint qe fair la peut
Et puis sey escuser soy voit.
Propre enbule le fait seluy,
Et le berzil bule auxi
La bracerez brewster en sa maner.
En droit del arte pluis nan'a afere,
Mais par taunt auetzavetz la seruoiseservoise
Dont aveint si bien a ease
Que les vnsuns pernent takys atauunt/
Par quey enyuerontenyveront drunkyn mayntenant sone/.
YueresseYveresse tient lais home clerke,
Homme mesceonu auoiravoir vnun merce.
YueresseYveresse fait coif/ de bricon foole
Rouge tient saunz vermyllon,
Et donke dit homme: parPar seint George,

l.said: Trop ad celuy bue/grossebue grosse dorged'orge.

lg.l: As tiels autours soy repose rist/,
Qe parler pense dautred'autre chose.
Si sauoirsavoir veulliez la manere
Come pescher fysh deurezdevrez en viuerviver,
ViuerViver est proprement nomee
Ou eawe/ viuevive quik est trouetrové,
Auqes also de ceruourcervour scop y espugez castowtcast owt
Si de pesson y querrez.
Et si vous faillez a l'estanke dame
Ou le eawe est adesadés currant/,
Alez donques saunz delay
Ou le eawe estoit en betenay myer,
Et illeoqe pescherez fyshe vous de nace syne
Ou petit rey net ne trouetrove grace,
Dont ilia·il i a nace &et creuerecrevere ridilnette auxi.
Pur cele aprise le vous di:

Qe le nace cyf est menument ouerezoverezOn the face of it, the double negative (les lytyll) in the gloss appears to contradict the sense of the French. There are signs that lytyll has been written in over an erasure. les lytyll holyd,
Mais pluis large perteuz hole assez
Ad la creuerecrevere rydyllnette, ppar qi le di·
Qe autre variaunce nadn'ad de luy.
Mais retournons a la matier,
Qe de pescher vous doi dire.
La gorgete throte du nace/ reuersezreversez surtorn·,
L'ordure fylth leinz engettez castowtcast owt/.
Crapaut, lesarde, si lelé trouetztrovetz,
Serpent ne coliovr snak ne esparniez.
GrauelGravel &et caillon flynt hors dettez;
Le limason sneyle tost descorchez/.
Et si de pesson i/i troueztrovez,
Par lez uambergezvambergez fyns pernez.
Et sils'il soit mulelwelle lyng de mere,
OuererOverer opyn le deuetzdevetz &et espander spand it.
Le no sunde vous en osterez;
Buwelle &et eschine, rygbane ensi le frez.
Et si de hesche huke i peschez fysshys,
Et de Rayray y trouereztroverez,This line has been executed into the margin, and incorporated into the main text through a marginal nota.
Marlin/, place/, plante de mer,
Esturgon, Saumonsaumon, turbaler turbot,
Le bresme du mer &et li porpeis,
Le planton &et li espineys thornbake,
Allose de mer en la seison,
Oistre, creviz &et cok hanon,
Le mulet/ ne ly makereauz
Et dorz smelt qe tauunt est douce &et beaus,
Le geleis &et le tauntepestauntepésTwo slashes connect the first 'e' to the 'p'. wylke

body.note: This folio contains an anathema, written in a later secretary hand over two lines perpendicular to the main text: Quisquis emit librum furto a quocumque receptum furtum committit est furti reus ergo: Whoever buys a book stolen from someone else is committing a crime, and therefore party to the theft..

lg.l: Qi par marine est meltz vseusé,
La laumpron &et la laumprie,
Et altres de mer salaye
Qe fortz strange serroit toutz a nomer.
Le harange nestn'est pas a voider,
Le gresset kungyr ne nene le carbonelle colefyssh/,
Ne luy chenet hundfyssh qe est nouellenovelle.
Mais daltred'altre pesson fait assauoirassavoir
Qe ne fait my a vblierublier:
La luce i/ai a premerement,
Perche &et roche ensement.
Menuse &et burbase i/ai a·;
D>D'anguille/, goiongojon ne faudra.
Auxi li auetzavetz li tendal
Qe par tout ne sont cominalOne of the minims has been inserted later.
Et si ad des petitz pessons
Qe en fraunceis appelent gernons tenchys,
Et des coans baynstekelyng i/ai a auxi.
Pur celle aprise le vous die:
Qe la coan est le pesson fyshe
Et la coane flyk ad la bacon.
Et quaunt suffyst la pecherye
En eawe fresse &et en salye,
Si retournons donques veier le Measonmeason
De garden vers cele creuisoncrevison gap
Tanques venez a verde tenail suarth·
Ou le pastour seiet oueové le aumail·,
Puis par celle boys en cele vmbraileumbraile
Passez pardesouth horail vmbyrumbyr.
Dont il iai a horail par ·h·.h. escript
Et orail saunz ·h·.h. escript/.
Desouth la horail soy tient laron thef,
Et de orail oist chescun home
Si de la tierd ersaper sap ne soit chargiechargié,
Ou loutre fylth ou glaue filth i/soiti soit entreentré/,
Ou autrement soit sourde devenu,
Par quoi l'oie heryng luy soit tolu takyn away.
Mais ains qe passe pluis auauntavaunt,
De tenail balk vous ert/ pluis disant/.
Il/i/aIl i a tenoun handille &et tenail,
Et tenails tangys dautred'autre aparail.
Et cotoursThe word cotours is written over a longer, erased word; judging from the space left on the line, this is likely to be a form of cotiver, in line with other MSS. tient le tenoun handil·,
Et par le tenail passe le prodhome.

body.note: Folio 16r has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 16v has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 17r has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 17v has been almost entirely lost.

Over half of Folio 20r has been torn, obscuring the latter part of most lines. Where possible, the end of the word 'in-progress' has been provided in the interpretive edition, but no attempt has been made to reconstruct the remainder of the missing lines.

lg.l: This section is acephalous.Et ambedeux sount
Puis qe ensemble sount
VncorUncor as floures
Qe ne sount d
Il y ad flour
Qe as oiles
Primerol &et primevere
Qe soy moustre
La counsyd dayse
Comen erbe
Et plantayn We have supplied a speculative resolution of the English gloss as waibrede, based on visible letters and the equivalent glosses in MSS ABCOTY. waibrede

The form ermois is visible up to four times on what remains of the lower half of this page (lines 573, 575, 577, and potentially 579). It is likely that this copy recorded a passage of homonym play not found in the other extant copies.Ermois
Qe le
Ermoi
Pur ben
Et er
Car i
Des

body.note: Folio 20v has been almost entirely lost.

lg.l: E plauntez cholettes wyrtes en lour lieu steede.

body.p:
Ore des arbres qe crescent
Enen gardeyn &et verger ensement/.

lg.l: En verger ou les arbres crescent,
Pur charge de fruit les vnsuns abeissent stowpes.

l.note: This term has been somewhat awkwardly corrected.

lg.l: Ceneler hautre qe cenele port,
Fourder slatre qe la fourdeyn porte/;
Eglenter espes port les peperounges chattes,
Et la creker bolastre les crekes bolas roundes.
Auxi auetzavetz la coigner coyntre
(SouentSovent la vist homme en verger)
Dont il y a difference graunt
Qe ne sceyuentsceyvent toutz a quauntaquaunt.
Le seignur fait son Naifnaif bondman coigner sytt/
En ses cippes stokkys pur chastier.
Et pernez le coigne bulax &et batez la coigner
Et coupez kutt du coigne vnun coignecoignér wedgys.
Et coignours dit homme ensement
Qe fount la monee dargentd'argent.
Autre arberie vous auetzavetz auxi.
Pur aprendre chescuny,
Buys y ad, palmere &et arable, box/ palmtre/ mapule/The glosses, unusually for this MS, are executed sequentially at the end of the line.
Bone est pur hanapes sauncz fable.
Et la huse y crest auxiholynThe target lemma for the gloss 'holyn' is indicated with two small slashes above huse.
Qe comoen est a chescuny.
Saunz leyn wylghs y crest keyn ak &et if ewe·,
De cest fraunceis est nul estrif.

Leyne, torcel, coudre, gaggerThe gloss waggys is not attested in MED as a tree name. However, waggys is found further up in MS 4, also in close proximity to coudre, as the verb gloss to croust. Has the scribe or exemplar been misled here by the repeat mention of coudre? waggys;
Pluis nyn'y affiert en vergier.

body.p: Ore en fraunceis pur toutz les oyseaux en boys.

lg.l: Quaunt de vergier auomsavoms les chose,
Ore alons iuerjuer en boys

Pur oier du russhinol nyghtgale la gale songe,gale is not attested in the AND, with gal_2, 'pleasure', the closest corresponding entry. It is possible (perhaps likely) that the scribe is attempting to regularise the unusual use (in most other MSS) of Middle English night[i]gale as part of the rhyme-scheme, and has attempted to reconstruct a plausible French ending to the sentence.
Car mieltz chauunt qe huwan oule em sale.
Et mielz chauunt mauueismauveis throstel en busshon
Qe chausorice fait en la maison. bake·
En braunche seet le menu merle litil vsilusilThese two glosses appear to have been added separately, but form a coherent single gloss.,
Et en marras demurt la herle skeldrake.
VncorUncor auetzavetz la filiaunde stare
Et l'oisel bride qadq'ad non chalaunde myrdrumbyllFor the gloss, see the compound mire-dromble under sense 4 of mire: the European bittern..
El forn maynt dwelys la salamaundre. creket

body.note: Most of this folio has been torn, obscuring the latter part of most lines. Where possible, the end of the word 'in-progress' has been provided in the interpretive edition, but no attempt has been made to reconstruct the remainder of the missing lines.

lg.l: Et les pessons fysshe
Eyles &et eales
Descordauntz
Car des eyles
Et des eales
Auxi il ad noer drunk
Dount la reson
En mer naent
Et en mer noent dru
Et des virons arys
En batelle bote le
Mais en yueryver veons
Et les aunfes flaws auavaler
Auxi il ad espandre
Paundre ensement
Cil espande spredys counseil
Que le descoueredescovere
Et lui emfs de
Hors dd'esquel sa

body.note: Most of the lines on folio 23v have been at least partially lost.

lg.l: ns dedé eyles paunde pessonLines 641-642 have been added in the margin.
Quaunt vif en reys le prenom
The visible letter from the main text hand, alongside evidence from other manuscripts, suggests that the word may have been forester.r
vient volant vnun rostelle wren·These lines have been added in the margin.

viroun vnun haut trosselleRothwell corrects this word in both MSS G and T to 'trestel', but concedes that no equivalent use of that word (to mean a stack of hay) is attested in Godefroy or Tobler-Lommatsch. Definition 4 under trestel in AND contains only variants of this line from the Tretiz and from the closely-derived Nominale sive Verbale, none of which supply the internal 't' that Rothwell adds here (the forms attested are tresel from MS G, tresselle from the Nominale, and trenceaus from O). AND also has a hypothetical headword [tasel] for what appears to be the same word used in MS T (line 272 of our edition). In both instances, a far more likely fit is provided by AND trusse (definition 2: truss of hay, etc.), with diminutive form trussel or tresel. stoke/.
Si renger voillez le mussenge.
Deslacez vngyrdeungyrde, vadlet, vostre renge gyrdyl.
VncorUncor il ad y paliuolpalivol nuthage
Ensement &et la chardonrole goldfynche.
Et si ad il des papillons butterfles
Qe voluntiers seent en chardons thystyls,
Et en les rounces breres souentsovent
VsentUsent seer naturelement/.
Mes ceo ne fait pas le grissillour gryshoper
QenQ'en courtilage gyrsgarth ad son soioursojour.
Et de herison vrchonurchon vous dirra plus
Qi prent le pommeThe final minim has been added above the line. qe chiet iusjus doun,
Musches flees ne wybeswybés gnattys ne le fount my,
Car ils ayment pluis la vrtyurty netyl·.
Auxi il ad ane en plumezoun
Qe en riuerriver ont lour maison.
En mores maignent les vanels wypes
Et en villes sont les venels lanys.
graunge vssonusson la vaune fane;
Entre enemys est la haiine hatredyn.
vient volant vnun roser wildeguse,
VnUn blarete a lui compaigner, a baldguse
Car mieltz valt vnun blarete peu
Qe ne ferroit la chare du freu ruk.
Et pluis est ignel swyft lui arounde swalow
Qe nul cicoigne stork parmy le mounde.
Et ceroundel a le cerounde hesyng
Prent les musshons &et l'arounde swalow.
Et le oysel qadq'ad a noun bekas snype
Pres de riuerriver est pris takyn en lace gyldyr.
La chaunt du cocoil cocowe est bien retous
Et si nestn'est gers noght delicious.
Mes pluis est oie en horiel porche
Que la voice du loriele wewale.
Et plus valt pel skyn de foun
Qe chaunt de asne ass ou de poun pacok.
Mais du poun la beale cowe taylle
EtEst delitable a le vewe,
Car plus ayme penne de poun
QenQ'en leschekerl'escheker mat de paun pawn.

body.p: Ore en fraunceis pur toutz les bestez sauuasauvages

lg.l: En fraunceis pluis auauntavaunt me voys
Pur veer les estres du boys.
Je veie venir vnun graund tesson brok
QadQ'ad guerpy forsakyn sa mansion
Pur les fimes dund'un gopil fox
Qe lui ad mys en exil flemyng.
Et hony shamyd soit le mauputoys, fulmarde
Ou soit en ville ou en boys,
Ne face force mes qilq'il eit
Gras gelyne a son reheit.
Et pluis valt le belet wesil
Pur fair ma graunge de rattez net
Que toutz les talpes moldewarp de cy fra hyn qapparysq'a Parys
Qe fount taupeneis moldewarp hils en pays.

body.p: Ore en fraunceis pur vnun charet od tut laffairl'affair qappentq'appent.

lg.l: Pur vneune charet a droit descrier
Le fraunceis vous met en liuerliver,
Dont les Roesroes vous dy an primer,
Et puis les bendeaux bandys qe sont de fer.
En les iauncesjaunces felis entrent les raes spokys,
EnuersEnvers le foir market vount les raies,
Et de la meer veignent les raies
Et du solail issent les raes.
Mais les raes du charet
En les moaux ount lour rescet. nafThis gloss is executed at the end of the line, and linked to the lemma with a pair of slashes.
Gist le moal dedentz le roef whele
Tant drot come moel del oef.
Jeo face lunl'un fort fes byrthyn a porter,
Et lautrel'autre bon a maunger.
Mais en les moaux gist l'essel axiltre
Que par deux hices pynnys se tient owel euynevyn.
Sur les asseaux axiltres gist le chartille,
Pur le sauuersauver de peril.There is a line of upside-down erased text here, as well as several pen-trials further down on the folio.
Les essels ount lour iunersjuners
Qe les eident come bon freres.
Si ert le chartille des bones brahelx wethys
Ferme liez as asseaux.
Et chescun char et qe mesne bleez
Doit auoiravoir ridels des coustez.
DuncostD'un cost le charcellechartelle sount lez ridels.
Sauncz clowes ficchesficchés roilons beles.
Entre le charcellechartelle &et lelé moalx
Sount hourtours troueztrovez deux.
Il y ad esselle &et asselle
Et le tierce vncoreuncore husselle·.
Le chartille lions sur les asselx;
En les moeaux sount les essealx.
Mais lelé haisseaux auietzavietz vous
En ambideux les braces desouth.
LesselL'essel fait bon Aa esteire
Od vnun dame en vile champestre
Pur le chaump saluersalver dd'aumail
Qe de gasterountdegasterount la semail.
Es limouns thyls vait luy lymoer thylhors
Qe a dorcere poort le dosser,
Et al ventre la ventrere,
Et au cow le waunere.
Ventre et ventrere y a,
DiuerseDiverse come vous orrez ya,
Ventrere est proprement nomenomé
VnUn femme qestq'est demuredemuré
Pur eidier en case sa vesine
Quaunt il gist en gesine;
Et quaunt il dd'enfauunt trauailletravaille
DuneD'une ventrere lors apparaile.
Mais proprement dit homme ventrere
Qe au ventre port limoere.

Le traicer ad le bracoroleThe gloss here is difficult. We suggest reading cod-ams, a compound that would be formed of cod (cf. MS O line 945, coddes) and (h)am, designating a wooden frame that embraces the horse; but this is only a tentative interpretation. codams
Qe le limours teigne par brace au cole.
En les braceroles sount billetz trepynnut
Tailetz du coteaux ou de hacchetz·.
Les colez des chiualxchivalx portent esteilez hamys,
Et colers de quire oueové lour hocels. berghames
En la charet est la somere,
La ou seit la charettere.
Et si la charetter ad son riort,
Dount sez chiualxchivalx en cours resort.
Donques ad quauntque a luy appent.
Assez suffist, qi que lententl'entent.
Mez pur estre pluis mieltz sachauuntsachaunt
Vous dirra eincz qe passe auauntavaunt
Le droit affair du charreter
Et ensement du charuere.
Le charitere estrile horscame aueraavera
Dount ses chiualxchivalx counroira
Et luy bouerebovere oxkepar ne le teigne a gaceIf ferde does, as we suggest, mean fear, the glossing here suggests that the scribe did not fully understand this expression. haldys to ferde
Qe bien ne froit sauncz turkas wysp.
Aincz qilsq'ils soient enbeiuerezenbeiverez
Serrount lez boefs deseruiezdeserviez, undun
Mais au soier late soient waiez watyrd

Et toutz autrez bestez lauiezlaviezA potential incorrect gloss. If related to haven, the scribe may have understood the French as l'aviez, from the verb to have; if linked instead to heven (to lift), perhaps the scribe misunderstood the French word as part of the verb lever?

body.note: Folio 25r has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 25v has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 26fr has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 26v has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 24r has been almost entirely lost, but the fragments visible correspond to ll. C 1018-25 (on building) followed by a rubric.
Folio 24v has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 27r has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 27v has been almost entirely lost.
Folio 28r has been almost entirely lost.

Folio 28v has been almost entirely lost. Based on a reconstruction of line numbering (see Appendix), it is probable that an additional folio has been lost between this and the current final folio (fol. 22 in the modern numbering)

Folio 22r has been almost entirely lost, but the fragments visible correspond to ll. C 1180-95, on physical ailments (from Hom clune des euz natureument to De mal usage prime le a). Based on a reconstruction of line numbering (see Appendix ), there may be a further lost folio which contained the end of the text.
Folio 22v has been almost entirely lost. Based on a reconstruction of line numbering (see Appendix), there may be a further lost folio which contained the end of the text.
